task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named baseline_network_performance.csv in /home/ga/SUMO_Output/ is visible in a file manager, terminal, or text editor, containing metrics for completed trips, average travel time, average time loss, and total vehicle-hours of delay.

task_completion.description: Create the incident rerouter definition closing at least two edges
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named incident_rerouters.add.xml in /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/ is visible, showing XML tags (like <rerouter> and <closingReroute> or <closedLaneReroute>) that specify the closure of at least two edges.

task_completion.description: Create the modified SUMO configuration for the incident
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named run_incident.sumocfg in /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/ is visible, showing the inclusion of the incident_rerouters.add.xml file in its configuration.

task_completion.description: Generate the incident performance metrics report
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named incident_network_performance.csv in /home/ga/SUMO_Output/ is visible, containing metrics that reflect the network's performance during the simulated incident.

A text file named incident_assessment_report.txt in /home/ga/SUMO_Output/ is visible, containing sections or text detailing the incident location, rerouting strategy, performance comparison, percentage degradation, and response recommendations.

integrity.description: Agent actually ran the SUMO simulation to generate metrics
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands executing 'sumo' or 'sumo-gui' with the respective configuration files, rather than the agent manually typing out fabricated CSV files.

integrity.description: Incident metrics show genuine network degradation compared to baseline
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in incident_network_performance.csv should show worse performance (e.g., higher travel times, higher delays, or fewer completed trips) than baseline_network_performance.csv, not identical or randomly improved numbers.

integrity.description: The assessment report contains actual analysis based on the simulation
integrity.visual_evidence: The incident_assessment_report.txt should contain specific numbers and percentages derived from the CSV files, rather than generic placeholder text or copied instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The baseline simulation yields approximately 8,779 completed trips, an average travel time of 276.18 seconds, an average time loss of 154.48 seconds, and 376.71 total vehicle-hours of delay. The agent must close at least 2 edges in incident_rerouters.add.xml. The incident simulation metrics must demonstrate plausible network degradation (e.g., increased travel time/delay or reduced completed trips) compared to the baseline.
